That misroutes tool scoping, dedup directives, and merge-preview logic away from the user’s actual intent. <div><a href="https://cursor.com/ope
candidates.replySummary: Applied. The counterparty merge follow-up classifier now rejects obvious non-counterparty merge domains, even after a recent duplicate-summary turn, and has regression coverage for invoice/transaction wording.

candidates.prTitle: fix(ai-chat): speed up duplicate counterparty review flows
candidates.title: Merge all ignores truncated duplicates
candidates.description: ### Merge all ignores truncated duplicates **Medium Severity** Semantic “merge all” previews only include duplicate groups parsed from `findDuplicates` results collected in the current turn. When the tool response is paginated (`truncated: true`), a single page load plus “merge all” still queues merges for that page only, while the user and system copy imply the full entity was merged. <div><a href="https://cursor.com/open?link=eyJ2ZXJzaW9uIjoxLCJ0eXBlIjoiQlVHQk9UX0ZJWF9JTl9DVVJTT1IiLCJkYXRhIjp7
candidates.replySummary: Applied. Semantic counterparty merge previews now fail closed when any collected `findDuplicates` result is still truncated, with coverage to prevent partial `merge all` previews.

candidates.prTitle: fix(ai-chat): speed up duplicate counterparty review flows
candidates.title: Paged merge blocked by truncation check
candidates.description: ### Paged merge blocked by truncation check **High Severity** Semantic counterparty merge previews bail out when any `counterparties.findDuplicates` result in the turn has `truncated: true`. After paging (page 1 still marked truncated, page 2 final), that guard stays true, so `buildSemanticCounterpartyMergePreviewWrites` returns nothing even when the model finished paging—blocking merge previews on large entities this PR targets. <div><a href="https://cursor.com/open?link=eyJ2ZXJzaW9uIjoxLCJ0eXB
candidates.replySummary: Applied. The preview builder now only blocks while the latest `findDuplicates` page is still truncated, so completed pagination can build merge previews from all collected pages.

candidates.replySummary: Applied. Initial duplicate-counterparty merge wording now stays in discovery mode unless there is recent duplicate-summary context, preserving the summarize-first flow.

candidates.description: **[SUGGESTION]:** Non-standard MIME type `image/tif` `image/tif` is not a registered IANA media type. TIFF files use `image/tiff`. The `.tif` extension should be mapped to the standard MIME type: ```suggestion 'image/tiff': ['.tiff', '.tif'], ``` As written, the `image/tif` MIME key is effectively unreachable since no browser assigns that type. Extension-based matching in the `accept` attribute should still work for `.tif` files, but this is fragile across different OS/browser combinations.

candidates.description: ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg) Unconditionally syncing the draft from `settings` whenever `settings` changes causes a major UX bug: when a user uploads a logo, `settings` is updated with the new logo URL, which triggers this effect and completely overwrites any unsaved changes the user has made to other fields (like `accentColor` or `footerMessage`). By resetting the draft to `null` when `entityId` changes and only initializing the draft when `draft` is `null`
candidates.replySummary: Good catch — real bug. Fixed: the draft now resets only on entity switch and initializes from settings only when it's null, so an out-of-band settings change (logo upload patching the cache) no longer clobbers unsaved edits.

candidates.title: settings payloads at the same time. If an older draft request reaches the backend after a newer one, it can persist stale branding defaults; the cache may correct itself while this
candidates.description: **P2** The debounced autosave can send multiple full `POST /invoice-settings` payloads at the same time. If an older draft request reaches the backend after a newer one, it can persist stale branding defaults; the cache may correct itself while this page stays mounted, but navigating away before that corrective save leaves the entity settings rolled back. Can we serialize/queue saves so the last user edit is the last backend write?
candidates.replySummary: Fixed in 937cc633. `useInvoiceBrandingDraft` now chains saves through a ref-backed promise queue, so overlapping autosaves execute sequentially and the latest user edit is the latest backend write.

candidates.title: Alpha slider creates a false affordance — value never propagates to consumer
candidates.description: **P3** — Alpha slider creates a false affordance — value never propagates to consumer The alpha slider (line 138–139) controls a local `alpha` state that only affects the trigger chip's background and the RGB/HSL readout tabs. `onChange` is never called with an alpha-adjusted value — the saved `accentColor` is always the fully opaque 6-digit hex (via `rgbToHex(hsvToRgb(...))` in `setColor` on line 147). Users who set alpha to 50% (or 0%) will see a different chip preview than the hex text beside
candidates.replySummary: Fixed in a1a19249. Removed the alpha slider/readouts because invoice branding persists a 6-digit opaque HEX color; the picker now only reflects and emits opaque HEX/RGB/HSL values.

candidates.title: SUGGESTION:** Missing required ARIA attributes for role="slider"
candidates.description: **SUGGESTION:** Missing required ARIA attributes for `role="slider"` WAI-ARIA requires `aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuemin`, and `aria-valuemax` on elements with `role="slider"`. The component declares `role="slider"` and provides `aria-valuetext` but omits the numeric value attributes, which screen readers rely on. Suggested attributes (lines 190-193): - `aria-valuenow` — the current value (e.g. `Math.round(hsv.s * 100)` for saturation or a composite) - `aria-valuemin="0"` - `aria-valuemax="100"` S
candidates.replySummary: Fixed in a1a19249. The saturation/brightness control now includes aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, and aria-valuemax while keeping aria-valuetext for the two-axis description.
